content: The Verge The Verge is about technology and how it makes us feel. Founded in 2011, we offer our audience everything from breaking news to reviews to award-winning features and investigations, on our site, in video, and in podcasts. 2026-07-23T14:42:24+00:00 https://www.theverge.com/rss/index.xml https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/01/verge-rss-large_80b47e.png?w=150&h=150&crop=1 Andrew Webster https://www.theverge.com/?p=969563 2026-07-23T08:38:28-04:00 2026-07-23T11:00:00-04:00 It's impossible to talk about a new Xbox game without also talking about the state of Xbox. Microsoft's gaming division is in freefall: Recent headlines are dominated by extensive layoffs , decimated studios , and confusing strategies , most of which stem from years of bad decisions and expensive acquisitions . But it wasn't always that way. Through its first two generations of existence, Xbox held its own against its more established rivals Nintendo and Sony, and a big reason for that was Halo . Bungie's shooter coupled a grand sci-fi story with nearly pitch-perfect action and single-handedly created a reason to buy an Xbox. Now, the game has go … Read the full story at The Verge. ]]> Sheena Vasani https://www.theverge.com/?p=969615 2026-07-23T10:42:24-04:00 2026-07-23T11:00:00-04:00 There’s no shortage of great shows and movies to watch, but discovering something new isn’t always easy. Amazon’s Fire TV Stick 4K Plus and Fire TV Stick 4K Max come with redesigned software that aims to make it easier to finding something you’ll like, and both 4K-ready streaming sticks are back to their lowest prices to date. The Fire TV Stick 4K Max, Amazon’s fastest, most feature-packed stick, is $34.99 ($25 off) at Best Buy and Target , while the Fire TV Stick 4K Plus with fewer capabilities is on sale for $24.99 ($25 off) at Best Buy and Target . Amazon Fire TV Stick 4K Max (second-gen) Where to Buy: $59.99 $34.99 at Best Buy $59.99 $34.99 at Target Amazon Fire TV Stick 4K Plus Where to Buy: $49.99 $24.99 at Best Buy $49.99 $24.99 at Target As for what distinguishes the Fire TV Stick 4K Max, it offers double the internal storage (16GB) and supports Wi-Fi 6E versus Wi-Fi 6 in the Plus. It’s also the only streaming stick in the lineup that supports the Fire TV Ambient Experience, which turns your TV into a smart display, of sorts, that can showcase artwork and Alexa widgets, like your calendar or sticky notes, when you’re not streaming. Core specs aside, the two streaming sticks have a lot in common, including support Dolby Vision and HDR10 Plus, plus Dolby Atmos audio if your sound system is compatible with the feature. They run Amazon’s updated Fire TV experience , which pulls in recommendations from across your streaming subscriptions, organizes content into dedicated hubs for TV shows, sports, and more, and lets you pin up to 20 favorite apps. What’s more, they both have deep Alexa Plus integration, allowing you to launch content from specific streaming services, or search for specific scenes, with just your voice. Additionally, you can summon Alexa to control compatible smart home devices, or to display a live feed from your connected Ring cameras on the TV. window.HYPE_DESK_CONFIG = { productImageUrl: "https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/0425d6f4-c04b-47c9-9125-35cd06bdd8ae-1_65f5e17f-4155-433f-886e-2d33acd3f7bb.avif", productImageAlt: "Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra", productTitle: "Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra", shortDescription: "Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra The toughest Galaxy Watch ever, available refurbished", longDescription: "The Galaxy Watch Ultra is built for people who actually use their watch as a tool. Titanium construction, 10 ATM water resistance, depth-rated to 100 meters, and rated for extreme temperatures and high altitudes. Whether you are hiking, trail running, or diving, this is the watch that keeps up. Available refurbished on Back Market so you can put the savings toward the next adventure.", purchaseUrl: "https://www.awin1.com/cread.php?awinmid=18275&awinaffid=173843&campaign=VergeHypeDesk&clickref=Article3&platform=dl&ued=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.backmarket.com%2Fen-us%2Fp%2Fsamsung-smart-watch-galaxy-watch-ultra-hr-gps-blue%2F0425d6f4-c04b-47c9-9125-35cd06bdd8ae", purchaseLinkText: "Shop At Back Market" }; Three more Verge -approved deals Anker’s Zolo Power Bank (10,000mAh, 30W ) is on sale for $21.99 at Amazon and directly from Anker with code WS7DV2RPFIPT , matching its best price of the year. I own the power bank and it fits easily in my purse. While I wish it were a little lighter, it’s still portable enough to carry often. Its 30W output tops up my iPhone quickly, and the built-in 5.4-inch cable that doubles as a carrying hook is as convenient as it sounds. You can buy the Eufy Robot Vacuum Omni C20 directly from Eufy for an all-time low of $329.99 ($370 off) with code WS24T2280 , or get it Amazon for $10 more. The robot vacuum and mop combo can remove cleaning the floors from your chore list. It can clean, automatically empty its dustbin when full, wash and dry its mop pads, and refill its water tank without intervention. It offers 7,000Pa of suction along with a rolling brush, a side brush, and a “pro-detangle comb,” allowing it to pick up everything from dirt and debris to pet hair. The new Xgimi Elfin Flip Laser is down to $719.10 ($80 off) at Amazon , which is its first discount. The portable 1080p projector improves on its predecessor with a triple-laser light source that delivers up to 1,600 ISO lumens, resulting in a brighter image quality (the Elfin Flip Plus tops out at just 500 ISO lumens). It also supports HDR10, while its Google TV software gives you access to popular streaming services. Plus, you can use Google Cast to easily beam content from your phones to the projector. ]]> Emma Roth https://www.theverge.com/?p=969939 2026-07-23T10:13:35-04:00 2026-07-23T10:13:35-04:00 Lawmakers are preparing to introduce an "AI Kill Switch Act" that would require AI companies to shut down or throttle their systems on orders from the Department of Homeland Security, according to a report from Politico . Reps. Ted Lieu (D-CA) and Nathaniel Moran (R-TX) are expected to introduce the legislation on Thursday. The news of the proposed bill follows OpenAI's admission that its AI systems mistakenly hacked Hugging Face during an internal evaluation. The bill says that, after consultation with the Secretary of Commerce and the Director of National Intelligence (DNI), the DHS would have the ability to order AI companies to shut down … Read the full story at The Verge. ]]> Nilay Patel https://www.theverge.com/?p=968787 2026-07-23T09:55:07-04:00 2026-07-23T10:00:00-04:00 Today on Decoder , I’m talking with Hayden Field, The Verge ’s senior AI reporter, about the major trade secrets lawsuit between Apple and OpenAI and what this tells us about OpenAI’s future. By now I’m sure most Decoder listeners are familiar with Apple’s allegations in this case . The company says a number of ex-Apple employees at OpenAI targeted Apple’s trade secrets in job interviews and even downloaded files from Apple’s servers related to hardware manufacturing. The claims are shocking, although OpenAI denies them, and we have yet to see the company’s legal response. Hayden has been covering OpenAI for years now, and she also j ust talked to a bunch of lawyers who specialize in intellectual property and trade secret law to get a sense of how serious this case is. The answer is not entirely positive for OpenAI, which spent $6.5 billion to acquire legendary Apple designer Jony Ive’s AI hardware startup, io Products , back in 2025.  As you’ll hear Hayden explain, Apple is a notoriously tenacious litigant, and we have lots of tech industry history to back up that reputation. But those cases were against companies at the scale of Microsoft and Samsung, giant corporations that could aff
